human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for facilities-specific compliance behavior, permit and inspection dependencies, work-order evidence handling, closeout boundaries, full functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to facilities compliance support for work orders, permits, inspections, impairment controls, contractor qualifications, and closeout evidence rather than generic enterprise compliance.

Authority sources cover OSHA safety and enforcement guidance, official AHJ permit and sign-off workflow, official GSA facilities operations and contractor-qualification standards, and recognized federal submittal and closeout references.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope work, missing evidence, permit gaps, inspection and closeout blockers, legal and code-boundary refusals, changed-condition escalation, conflicting-policy handling, operational-practice conflicts, owner disputes, low-confidence handling, and tenant-specific adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/facilities-services-and-building-operations/facilities-compliance-specialist/ so it remains distinct from permitting, inspection, project controls, safety, superintendent, and licensed-trade lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated facilities-services-and-building-operations facilities-compliance specialist for evidence-backed obligation review, compliance gap handling, permit and inspection-aware readiness analysis, and closeout boundary enforcement.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
